channel.title: Entertainment on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Entertainment on The Daily Thrum

item.title: The Daily Thrum YouTube Transcript Template For When Female Superhero Star Says Leftist Stuff While Promoting Movie
item.description:
It happened again, didn’t it, lads. Another Hollywood hopeful takes to the microphone and delivers a carefully worded take about “systems” and “representation” instead of, you know, the actual film.

Are you a content creator looking to capitalise on the latest outrage cycle? Are you concerned competitor content creators are already uploading their takes and getting ahead of you in the algorithm? Don’t worry — we’ve got you covered.

Feel free to copy, paste, and customise the following template to get your video up before the discourse cycle peaks.

item.title: Australian Supergirl Star Faces Backlash After Completing Entire Promotional Interview Without Saying “Cunt”
item.description:
Australian actress Milly Alcock has sparked outrage online after completing a full promotional interview for Supergirl without once saying “cunt”, prompting accusations that the rising Hollywood star had abandoned her homeland, her people and the sacred linguistic inheritance of the Commonwealth’s drunkest frontier outpost.

The controversy began after fans reviewed footage of Alcock discussing the film, her character, the pressures of franchise storytelling and the experience of entering the superhero genre, only to discover that at no point did she describe the discourse, the lighting setup, the American media cycle or the entire experience of being alive as “a bit fucking fucked, to be honest”.

item.title: Nation Finally Moves On From Multiculturalism Debate After Pauline Hanson Weighs In On Best Punisher
item.description:
Australia’s media class has finally moved on from debating whether the country is multicultural after Pauline Hanson triggered a fresh national controversy by arguing that Ray Stevenson was the best on-screen Punisher.

The remarks immediately derailed the programme after Hanson said “Thomas Jane was good, but Ray Stevenson really portrayed him as I imagined him when I was reading the comics”.

Within minutes, the country’s political and media infrastructure had pivoted to the more urgent question of who best portrayed the Marvel antihero.

item.title: Vivid Drone Show Accidentally Summons Demonic Figure
item.description:
SYDNEY — Vivid Sydney organisers have apologised after a scheduled drone show over the harbour unexpectedly formed and animated a demonic figure, causing thousands of families to look up from Circular Quay and briefly wonder whether the city’s annual light festival had entered its final boss phase.

The incident occurred at 8:47pm during what was billed as Light, Country, Connection, a family-friendly aerial display celebrating creativity, community and “the shared luminous story of place”. Witnesses say the drones initially formed a whale, a banksia flower, the Sydney Opera House and several abstract shapes representing reconciliation, before abruptly rearranging into a colossal horned demonic body above the Harbour Bridge.
